title: M006-E004-S001: Extend measurement spec types for entity definitions
description:
## Story: M006-E004-S001

Add EntityDefinition and EntityProperty interfaces to types.ts. Extend MeasurementSpec with optional entities field and TrackingEvent with optional entity_id. Backwards compatible.

### Acceptance Criteria
- EntityProperty: name, type (string|number|boolean|array), description, isRequired
- EntityDefinition: id, name, description, properties[]
- MeasurementSpec includes optional entities: EntityDefinition[]
- TrackingEvent includes optional entity_id: string
- Types align with measurementPlan.ts schema shape
- Existing specs without entities still type-check
- Existing TrackingEvent without entity_id still type-checks

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/schema.ts
- convex/measurementPlan.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Fix both pre-existing type/runtime mismatches (EventProperty.isRequired, coverage sub-object) alongside new entity types — types catching up to reality
- Keep EntityProperty and EventProperty as separate interfaces (different domain concepts: entity schema vs event payload)
- EntityDefinition.id is LLM-generated string identifier, not a Convex DB reference
- Use strict type union for EntityProperty.type matching existing EventProperty pattern

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer questioned EntityDefinition on MeasurementSpec (separation of concerns) — rejected per AC requirements
- Reviewer suggested merging EntityProperty/EventProperty — rejected per AC, different domain concepts
- Valid point: three type systems need alignment — this story fixes two existing drift points
- No unnecessary abstractions — just interfaces and optional fields

### Final Design
Add EntityProperty and EntityDefinition interfaces to types.ts. Add isRequired to EventProperty. Restructure MeasurementSpec coverage to sub-object. Add optional entities and entity_id fields. Update parser to use isRequired. Update tests.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-entity-types-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: (1) types.ts changes, (2) parser fix in generateMeasurementSpec.ts (required→isRequired), (3) test updates for both existing and new types

### Implementation Tasks

description:
## Mission: M008 Open Source Foundation

WebsiteCrawler crawls marketing site — homepage, features, about, help/docs. Classifies page types, extracts clean text from HTML.

title: Extract product outcomes from marketing content
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile

**Story M001-E001-S005:** Extract outcomes/jobs-to-be-done from hero messaging, value props, and testimonials.

## Specification

A Convex action analyzes homepage hero messaging, value propositions, and customer testimonials to identify the outcomes the product delivers — primary and secondary jobs-to-be-done, linked to features where possible.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] Outcomes extracted as array of { description, type, linkedFeatures[] } — type is primary/secondary/tertiary
2. [unit] Primary outcome captures the main job-to-be-done (e.g., 'collaborate visually in real-time')
3. [unit] Outcomes are linked to specific features mentioned on the site where possible
4. [unit] Evidence includes URLs and excerpts from hero sections, value props, testimonials
5. [integration] Result stored via updateSection(productId, 'outcomes', data) matching schema

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Classify outcome type by positioning hierarchy (hero=primary, value-props=secondary, testimonial-only=tertiary)
- Reflects company's own prioritization; more objective than business impact inference
- Product leaders want clean positioning data to make their own judgments

### Technical Perspective
- Single Haiku call with labeled page sections (homepage, features, customers)
- Follows established S001-S004 pattern: internalAction → listByProductInternal → Haiku → updateSectionInternal
- Feature linkage as string array from marketing copy, not structured entity reference
- ~25KB truncation per page, flat evidence array

### Simplification Review
- Tightened outcome type definitions to one line each
- Confirmed single-call approach sufficient for surface-level marketing copy
- No need to read existing profile sections (unlike S004)

### Final Design
Single internalAction with one Haiku call. Fetches homepage/features/customers pages, extracts outcomes classified by positioning hierarchy, links to feature names from marketing copy, stores via updateSectionInternal.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-product-outcomes-design.md

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-product-outcomes-impl.md

### Overview
Single internalAction with one Claude Haiku call. Fetches crawled homepage/features/customers pages, extracts outcomes classified by positioning hierarchy, links to feature names, stores via updateSectionInternal.

### Tasks

- Write failing tests for extractOutcomes (7 test cases covering all acceptance criteria)
- Implement extractOutcomes internalAction with prompt, JSON parsing, evidence stripping
- Verify types, lint, and full test suite

title: M006-E004-S003: Update MeasurementSpecSection UI for entity-grouped events
description:
## Story: M006-E004-S003

Add entity-grouped view to MeasurementSpecSection. Entity cards with name, description, property table, and nested events. Toggle between entity and category views. Category view is fallback for legacy data.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Entity view rendered by default when entities exist
- Each entity renders as card with name, description, event count badge
- Entity card includes property table (name, type, required, description)
- Events nested under parent entity using existing EventRow component
- Toggle between 'By Entity' and 'By Category' views
- Category view unchanged from current implementation
- Legacy data (no entities) shows category view, toggle hidden
- Summary bar shows entity count alongside event count
- Events without entity_id shown in 'Ungrouped' section

### Key Files
- src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.tsx
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Toggle right-aligned on same line as summary bar — perspective shift on same dataset, not navigation
- Confidence score stays visible in both views — trust in data, not grouping
- Summary bar shows entity count alongside event count when entities present
- The magic moment is the seamless perspective shift when toggling between entity and category views

### Technical Perspective
- Simple two-button toggle (not Radix Tabs) — different mental model from page-level tab navigation
- ~10 lines inline JSX: bg-gray-100 container, white + shadow-sm for active state
- Always-visible property table — entity properties define what an entity *is*, hiding behind collapse contradicts entity-first mental model
- Reuse EventRow as-is in both views — visual consistency, no modifications needed
- Inline event filtering (simple filter() calls) rather than Map-based helper

### Simplification Review
- Removed separate component extractions (CategorySummaryBar, EntitySummaryBar, ViewToggle) — inline as JSX
- Removed Phase 1 refactoring — build entity view directly
- Kept always-visible property table (core to entity-first mental model)
- Kept Required column in property table (explicit acceptance criterion)
- Kept entity count in summary bar (explicit acceptance criterion)
- Inline event grouping logic — simple filter() calls rather than Map-based helper
- Entity cards stay as local function for readability

### Final Design
Entity cards with always-visible property schemas and nested EventRows. Inline two-button toggle on summary bar for view switching. Confidence visible in both views. Legacy fallback to category view. All in MeasurementSpecSection.tsx.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-spec-entity-ui-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: viewMode state, entity card rendering, property table, event filtering per entity, toggle, summary bar updates, ungrouped section, tests (9 cases)

title: M006-E001-S001: Replace TF-IDF clustering with LLM-based semantic clustering
description:
## Story: M006-E001-S001

Replace clusterCandidatesCore (TF-IDF cosine similarity) with an LLM-based clustering function that sends all validated candidates to Claude for semantic grouping. Keep TF-IDF as fallback.

### Acceptance Criteria
- New clusterCandidatesLLM function accepts ValidatedCandidate[] and returns CandidateCluster[]
- No cluster contains two candidates from the same lens
- Parsing handles JSON in code fences and raw JSON
- Fallback to TF-IDF clustering if LLM call fails
- Prompt includes same-lens constraint as explicit rule
- Prompt requests 15-30 clusters as target range
- runConvergencePipeline uses LLM clustering instead of TF-IDF by default

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/clusterCandidates.ts (add clusterCandidatesLLM)
- convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts (wire new clustering)
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Single-call approach is simplest: ~70 candidates fits in one Claude call (~2,050 input tokens)
- Per-lens clustering + merge rejected: 7 LLM calls + merge logic adds code/latency/failure modes for no benefit at this scale
- Post-hoc same-lens validation with repair (split violations into singletons) -- pragmatic, 3 lines of code
- Reuse Anthropic client already created in runConvergencePipeline -- just move creation earlier
- Use extractJson from shared.ts for JSON parsing consistency

### Simplification Review
- Design approved as minimal and inevitable
- Repair strategy justified: rare edge case, trivial code, more resilient than retry loops
- No hidden bloat -- single-call architecture sidesteps per-lens coordination complexity

### Final Design
Four new exports in clusterCandidates.ts: CLUSTERING_SYSTEM_PROMPT, buildClusteringPrompt, parseClusteringResponse, clusterCandidatesLLM. Wire into convergeAndTier.ts with try/catch: LLM first, TF-IDF fallback. Same-lens repair + orphan handling for resilience. No changes to types.ts.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-llm-semantic-clustering-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: system prompt text, buildClusteringPrompt format, parseClusteringResponse validation/repair logic, clusterCandidatesLLM function, convergeAndTier.ts wiring changes, test cases for parsing/repair/orphans/fallback

title: Implement convergence and tiering
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E002:** Validation & Convergence - Clean and cluster lens output

**Story M003-E002-S004:** Merge clusters into named value moments with tier assignment.

**Dependencies:** Requires S003 (clustering) to exist.

## Specification

An LLM-assisted convergence function merges each cluster into a single named
value moment, combining insights from all contributing lenses. Moments are
tiered by lens count: 5+ = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] convergeAndTier function accepts CandidateCluster[] and returns ValueMoment[]
2. [unit] Each cluster is merged into a single ValueMoment using LLM
3. [unit] Value moment name follows format: verb + what user gets
4. [unit] Clusters with 5+ lenses are assigned Tier 1
5. [unit] Clusters with 3-4 lenses are assigned Tier 2
6. [unit] Clusters with 1-2 lenses are assigned Tier 3
7. [integration] Linear produces 4-6 Tier 1 moments
8. [integration] Total value moments for Linear is 15-30
9. [integration] Merged descriptions combine insights from all contributing lenses
10. [integration] Each moment has contributing_candidates for traceability

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts as an internalAction.

LLM prompt merges candidate insights into single named moment with verb + outcome format.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts
- convex/analysis/convergence/clusterCandidates.ts

---
*Product Story: M003-E002-S004 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Trust upstream clustering (0.7 similarity threshold) — no split logic for V1
- Log warnings if LLM flags incoherent clusters, but always merge
- "Verb + outcome" naming is the key user-facing quality signal
- Ship and iterate: add split handling only if real data shows it's needed

### Technical Perspective
- Per-cluster LLM calls with Promise.allSettled — matches orchestrate.ts patterns
- Each cluster is a unit of work: merge it, tier it, store it
- Pure functions for testability: assignTier, parseMergeResponse
- Fallback to directMerge on LLM failure to avoid data loss
- Model: claude-sonnet (merge quality needs judgment)

### Simplification Review
- Removed tier-based merge branching: call LLM uniformly for all clusters
- Removed testRunConvergencePipeline: premature API surface for unwired code
- Inlined buildMergePrompt and dedupeStrings: no abstractions for one-time use
- Kept assignTier and parseMergeResponse as pure functions (genuine testing value)

### Final Design
convergeAndTier(clusters, client) iterates all clusters uniformly via Promise.allSettled, calls Claude Sonnet per cluster with verb+outcome prompt, parses JSON response, assigns tier by lens count, falls back to directMerge on failure. runConvergencePipeline internalAction orchestrates validate → cluster → converge → store.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: pure functions with tests, LLM merge prompt, convergeAndTier core function, runConvergencePipeline internalAction.

**Design doc:** docs/plans/2026-02-07-convergence-and-tiering-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Implement `convergeAndTier.ts` — an LLM-assisted convergence function that merges each `CandidateCluster` into a named `ValueMoment` with tier assignment (5+ lenses = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3). The function calls Claude Sonnet per cluster via `Promise.allSettled`, parses verb+outcome named moments, and falls back to `directMerge` on LLM failure. A `runConvergencePipeline` internalAction orchestrates the full validate → cluster → converge → store flow.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1: `convergeAndTier` function in `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts` accepts `CandidateCluster[]` and an Anthropic client, returns `ValueMoment[]`
- [ ] AC2: Each cluster triggers one LLM call to `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` via the Anthropic client (verifiable via mocked client call count in tests)
- [ ] AC3: Each returned `ValueMoment.name` starts with a verb (e.g., "Gain", "Reduce", "Accelerate") — validated by `parseMergeResponse` throwing on non-verb names
- [ ] AC4: `assignTier(5)` returns `1`, `assignTier(7)` returns `1`
- [ ] AC5: `assignTier(3)` returns `2`, `assignTier(4)` returns `2`
- [ ] AC6: `assignTier(1)` returns `3`, `assignTier(2)` returns `3`
- [ ] AC7: (Integration — deferred) Linear produces 4-6 Tier 1 moments when full pipeline is wired
- [ ] AC8: (Integration — deferred) Total value moments for Linear is 15-30
- [ ] AC9: Each `ValueMoment.description` contains text referencing contributing lenses (verifiable in unit test with mocked LLM returning multi-lens description)
- [ ] AC10: Each `ValueMoment.contributing_candidates` array contains the `id` values of all candidates in the source cluster

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create `convergeAndTier.ts` with pure functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Export `assignTier(lens_count: number): ValueMomentTier` — pure tier mapping: `>=5 → 1`, `>=3 → 2`, else `3`
   - Export `parseMergeResponse(text: string): { name: string; description: string; roles: string[]; product_surfaces: string[]; is_coherent: boolean }` — extract JSON from code fences (regex: `/\`\`\`(?:json)?\s*\n?([\s\S]*?)\n?\s*\`\`\`/`), validate required fields (`name`, `description`, `roles`, `product_surfaces`, `is_coherent`), throw on missing fields. Follow `parseIdentityResponse` pattern from `convex/analysis/extractIdentity.ts`

2. **Add `directMerge` fallback function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Export `directMerge(cluster: CandidateCluster): ValueMoment` — constructs a ValueMoment without LLM by: concatenating candidate names with " / " as the moment name prefixed with "Achieve" (verb), joining descriptions, deduping roles and product_surfaces via `Set`, setting tier via `assignTier(cluster.lens_count)`, collecting `contributing_candidates` from `cluster.candidates.map(c => c.id)`

3. **Add `MERGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and core `convergeAndTier` function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - `MERGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`: instruct Claude to merge candidates into one named value moment, name MUST start with verb (Gain, Reduce, Accelerate, etc.), combine insights from ALL lenses, return JSON in code fences with fields: `name`, `description`, `roles`, `product_surfaces`, `is_coherent`
   - Export async `convergeAndTier(clusters: CandidateCluster[], client: Anthropic): Promise<ValueMoment[]>` with Promise.allSettled pattern, parseMergeResponse + assignTier per result, directMerge fallback on failure
   - Use model `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` and `max_tokens: 4096` matching orchestrate.ts pattern

4. **Add `runConvergencePipeline` internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Orchestrates: instantiate Anthropic client → cluster candidates → converge and tier → build ConvergenceResult → store via updateSectionInternal

5. **Write unit tests for pure functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts`
   - Tests for `assignTier` (AC4, AC5, AC6), `parseMergeResponse` (AC3), `directMerge` (AC10)

6. **Write unit tests for `convergeAndTier` with mocked Anthropic client**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts`
   - Tests for AC1 (input/output types), AC2 (one LLM call per cluster), AC9 (lens descriptions), AC10 (contributing_candidates), fallback behavior

### Test Plan

- [ ] `assignTier` tests verify AC4, AC5, AC6
- [ ] `parseMergeResponse` tests verify JSON extraction from code fences and field validation

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Read stored fields rather than recomputing: completeness and overallConfidence are write-time computed aggregates maintained by updateSectionInternal — this is a synchrony pattern, not caching
- O(1) per product for profile summary reads via by_product index
- If drift is a concern, validate with tests rather than recomputing in the query

### Simplification Review
- Design is minimal and inevitable — no bloat, no unnecessary abstractions
- Single ~25-30 line query function is the irreducible essence of the requirement

### Final Design
Add listWithProfiles query to convex/products.ts:
1. Reuse products.list auth pattern (Clerk identity, user lookup, by_user index)
2. For each product, fetch profile via by_product index
3. Return { ...product, profile: { completeness, overallConfidence, hasConvergence: !!convergence, hasOutputs: !!outputs } | null }

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover the query function and convex-test unit tests
- Tests should cover: auth user gets products, profile summary fields, null profile case, auth pattern reuse

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Goal
Add `listWithProfiles` query to `convex/products.ts` that returns products joined with profile summary data, eliminating N+1 queries on the product list page.

### Current State
- `convex/products.ts:30-46` has `products.list` query — auth pattern to reuse (Clerk identity → user lookup by `by_clerk_id` → products by `by_user` index)
- `convex/productProfiles.ts` stores profiles with `by_product` index, `completeness` and `overallConfidence` as write-time computed fields
- `convergence` and `outputs` are dynamic fields on productProfiles (stored via `updateSectionInternal` but not in strict schema) — must cast profile to `Record<string, unknown>` to access
- `convex/products.test.ts` has established test patterns: `setupUser()`, `authenticatedUser()` helpers using `convex-test`

### Changes

#### 1. Add `listWithProfiles` query to `convex/products.ts`

Insert after line 47 (after the existing `list` query). ~25 lines:

```typescript
export const listWithProfiles = query({
  args: {},
  handler: async (ctx) => {
    const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity();
    if (!identity) return [];

    const user = await ctx.db
      .query("users")
      .withIndex("by_clerk_id", (q) => q.eq("clerkId", identity.subject))
      .first();
    if (!user) return [];

    const products = await ctx.db
      .query("products")
      .withIndex("by_user", (q) => q.eq("userId", user._id))
      .collect();

    return Promise.all(
      products.map(async (product) => {
        const profileDoc = await ctx.db
          .query("productProfiles")
          .withIndex("by_product", (q) => q.eq("productId", product._id))
          .first();

        if (!profileDoc) {
          return { ...product, profile: null };
        }

        const profileAny = profileDoc as Record<string, unknown>;
        return {
          ...product,
          profile: {
            completeness: profileDoc.completeness,
            overallConfidence: profileDoc.overallConfidence,
            hasConvergence: !!profileAny.convergence,
            hasOutputs: !!profileAny.outputs,
          },
        };
      })
    );
  },
});
```

#### 2. Add tests to `convex/products.test.ts`

Add a `describe("listWithProfiles")` block with 4 tests:

1. **Returns products for authenticated user** — create 2 products, call `listWithProfiles`, assert length 2 and product fields present
2. **Includes profile summary fields** — create product + profile with identity section (to get non-zero completeness), call `listWithProfiles`, assert `profile.completeness`, `profile.overallConfidence`, `profile.hasConvergence`, `profile.hasOutputs` are present
3. **Returns profile: null for products without profile** — create product without profile, assert `profile` is `null`
4. **Returns empty for unauthenticated user** — call without auth, assert `[]`

Test data setup:
- Use existing `setupUser()` and `authenticatedUser()` helpers
- For profile tests: insert directly into `productProfiles` table via `t.run()` with `productId`, `completeness`, `overallConfidence`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
- For convergence/outputs tests: use `t.run()` to patch profile with dynamic fields

### Testing
```bash
npm test -- convex/products.test.ts
```

All 4 acceptance criteria map to unit tests. No manual testing needed.

### Acceptance Verification
- [x] Query added to `convex/products.ts` after `list`
- [x] Auth pattern identical to `products.list` (returns `[]` for unauth)
- [x] Profile fetched via `by_product` index
- [x] Returns `{ completeness, overallConfidence, hasConvergence, hasOutputs }` or `null`
- [x] 4 passing unit tests in `convex/products.test.ts`

6 value moments (REF-01 through REF-06): Keyboard-First Speed, Cycle Completion with Team Visibility [PRIMARY], Smart Triage Workflow, Roadmap-to-Issue Traceability, Development Tool Integration, Ambient Team Awareness. M002 mapping table at end.

title: Add unit tests for output type validation
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E001:** Output Types Schema

**Story M004-E001-S002:** Add unit tests for output type validation

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E001-S001 (output types)

## Specification

Unit tests verify that output types are properly defined and that type guards and validation helpers work correctly for runtime type checking.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] Test file exists at convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts
2. [unit] Tests verify ICPProfile required fields are present
3. [unit] Tests verify ActivationMap stages array structure
4. [unit] Tests verify MeasurementSpec events array structure
5. [unit] Tests verify TrackingEvent maps_to field validation
6. [unit] Type guard functions isICPProfile, isActivationMap, isMeasurementSpec are tested

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.test.ts

---
*Product Story: M004-E001-S002 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Type guards belong in separate `guards.ts` file — keeps `types.ts` as pure interfaces (no runtime code)
- Mental model: "types define shapes, guards validate instances"
- Shallow structural field checks (2-3 discriminating fields per guard) are sufficient; deep validation is generator responsibility
- Guards are reusable by downstream generators that parse LLM JSON output

### Simplification Review
- Reduced test structure from 7 describe blocks to 4-5 — compile-time type verification happens naturally when constructing test objects for guard tests
- Kept guards in `guards.ts` since acceptance criteria explicitly requires them and downstream stories depend on them
- No deep validation in guards — generators handle their own deeper checks

### Final Design
Two files:
1. `convex/analysis/outputs/guards.ts` — three type guard functions (`isICPProfile`, `isActivationMap`, `isMeasurementSpec`) with shallow structural checks
2. `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts` — 4-5 describe blocks combining type structure verification with guard tests

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover:
- Exact test cases per describe block
- Guard function signatures and field checks
- Test data fixtures (valid objects, invalid objects, edge cases)
- Integration with vitest test runner

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-08-output-type-tests-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Create runtime type guard functions in `convex/analysis/outputs/guards.ts` and comprehensive tests in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`. Guards perform shallow structural validation (field existence + type checks) for ICPProfile, ActivationMap, and MeasurementSpec — used by downstream generators to validate LLM JSON output. Tests verify both compile-time type construction and runtime guard behavior.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: File `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts` exists and `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts` exits with code 0
- [ ] AC2: `types.test.ts` contains a `describe("ICPProfile")` block that constructs a valid ICPProfile object with all required fields (`id`, `name`, `description`, `value_moment_priorities`, `activation_triggers`, `pain_points`, `success_metrics`, `confidence`, `sources`) and asserts each field's value
- [ ] AC3: `types.test.ts` contains a `describe("ActivationMap")` block that constructs a valid ActivationMap with a `stages` array containing at least one ActivationStage with fields `level`, `name`, `signal_strength`, `trigger_events`, `value_moments_unlocked`, `drop_off_risk`, and asserts the stages array structure
- [ ] AC4: `types.test.ts` contains a `describe("MeasurementSpec")` block that constructs a valid MeasurementSpec with an `events` array containing at least one TrackingEvent with fields `name`, `description`, `properties`, `trigger_condition`, `maps_to`, `category`, and asserts the events array structure
- [ ] AC5: `types.test.ts` contains a `describe("TrackingEvent maps_to")` block that constructs TrackingEvent objects for all three discriminated union variants (`{ type: "value_moment" }`, `{ type: "activation_level" }`, `{ type: "both" }`) and asserts the `type` field on each
- [ ] AC6: File `convex/analysis/outputs/guards.ts` exports three functions: `isICPProfile(value: unknown): value is ICPProfile`, `isActivationMap(value: unknown): value is ActivationMap`, `isMeasurementSpec(value: unknown): value is MeasurementSpec`
- [ ] AC7: Each guard function is tested with: (a) a valid object → returns `true`, (b) `null` → returns `false`, (c) a non-object (string/number) → returns `false`, (d) an object missing key fields → returns `false`

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create guards file with three type guard functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/guards.ts`
   - Change: Import types from `./types`. Implement three type guard functions:
     - `isICPProfile`: checks `value` is object, has `name` (string), `value_moment_priorities` (Array), `activation_triggers` (Array)
     - `isActivationMap`: checks `value` is object, has `stages` (Array), `primary_activation_level` (number)
     - `isMeasurementSpec`: checks `value` is object, has `events` (Array), `total_events` (number)
   - Pattern: Each guard returns `value is T` (TypeScript type predicate). Use `typeof` + `Array.isArray` for checks. No deep validation.

2. **Create test file with ICPProfile describe block**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Import types from `./types` and guards from `./guards`. Add `describe("ICPProfile")` with:
     - `it("defines all required fields")` — construct valid ICPProfile, assert each field
     - `it("isICPProfile returns true for valid object")` — pass valid object to guard
     - `it("isICPProfile returns false for null, non-object, missing fields")` — test invalid inputs
   - Pattern: Follow `convex/analysis/convergence/types.test.ts` style (construct typed objects, assert field values)

3. **Add ActivationMap describe block**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Add `describe("ActivationMap")` with:
     - `it("defines stages array with ActivationStage structure")` — construct valid ActivationMap with populated stages, assert stage fields
     - `it("includes transitions and primary_activation_level")` — assert transitions array and primary_activation_level field
     - `it("isActivationMap returns true for valid object")` — pass valid object to guard
     - `it("isActivationMap returns false for invalid inputs")` — test null, non-object, missing stages

4. **Add MeasurementSpec describe block**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Add `describe("MeasurementSpec")` with:
     - `it("defines events array with TrackingEvent structure")` — construct valid MeasurementSpec with populated events, assert event fields
     - `it("includes coverage fields")` — assert `total_events`, `activation_levels_covered`, `value_moments_covered`
     - `it("isMeasurementSpec returns true for valid object")` — pass valid object to guard
     - `it("isMeasurementSpec returns false for invalid inputs")` — test null, non-object, missing events

5. **Add TrackingEvent maps_to describe block**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Add `describe("TrackingEvent maps_to")` with:
     - `it("supports value_moment variant")` — construct TrackingEvent with `maps_to: { type: "value_moment", moment_id: "vm-001" }`, assert `maps_to.type === "value_moment"`
     - `it("supports activation_level variant")` — construct with `maps_to: { type: "activation_level", activation_level: 3 }`, assert type
     - `it("supports both variant")` — construct with `maps_to: { type: "both", moment_id: "vm-001", activation_level: 3 }`, assert type and both fields present

### Test Plan

- [ ] Run `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts` — exits with code 0, all tests pass
- [ ] AC2 verified by: ICPProfile describe block constructs object with all 9 required fields and asserts each
- [ ] AC3 verified by: ActivationMap describe block constructs object with stages array containing ActivationStage with all required fields
- [ ] AC4 verified by: MeasurementSpec describe block constructs object with events array containing TrackingEvent with all required fields
- [ ] AC5 verified by: TrackingEvent maps_to describe block tests all 3 discriminated union variants
- [ ] AC6 verified by: guards.ts exports three type-predicate functions imported in test file
- [ ] AC7 verified by: Each guard tested with valid object (true), null (false), string (false), missing-fields object (false)

---

title: Orchestrate analysis pipeline after scan completion
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile

**Story M001-E001-S007:** Wire all analysis extractors into an orchestration pipeline triggered after scan completes.

**Dependencies:** Requires S001-S006 (all extractors) to exist so they can be invoked.

## Specification

When a scan completes, an orchestration action automatically triggers all analysis extractors, runs independent ones in parallel, chains dependent ones sequentially, creates the profile record, and marks it as generated.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [integration] Orchestration action is triggered after scanJobs.complete() sets status to 'complete'
2. [integration] Profile record is created via productProfiles.create(productId) if it doesn't exist
3. [integration] Independent extractors run in parallel: identity, revenue, entities, outcomes
4. [integration] Dependent extractors run after prerequisites: journey+definitions after identity, metrics after identity+revenue
5. [integration] All section results are stored via updateSection mutations
6. [integration] Final completeness and overallConfidence are recalculated after all sections stored
7. [integration] scanJob status is updated to reflect analysis phase (e.g., 'analyzing' → 'complete')
8. [integration] Errors in individual extractors don't block other extractors — partial profiles are acceptable
9. [integration] Total analysis completes within Convex 10-minute action limit for a typical site

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-04-orchestrate-analysis-pipeline-impl.md`

### Overview
Single internalAction orchestrator triggered via scheduler.runAfter(0) after scan completes. Creates profile, runs 4 independent extractors in parallel (identity, revenue, entities, outcomes), then 2 dependent extractors (journey, metrics). Each extractor stores its own section. Errors isolated via Promise.allSettled.

### Tasks
7 TDD tasks covering:
- Add listByProductInternal to crawledPages
- Add createInternal + getInternal to productProfiles
- Add updateSectionInternal to productProfiles
- Document analyzing/analyzed status flow in scanJobs tests
- Create orchestrator action (convex/analysis/orchestrate.ts)
- Wire orchestrator trigger in scanning.ts
- Full test suite verification

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

title: Define lens candidate schema and types
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E001:** Lens Execution Pipeline - Build 7 analytical lenses

**Story M003-E001-S001:** Define the common output structure for all 7 analytical lenses.

## Specification

TypeScript types and Convex schema define the common LensCandidate structure
used by all 7 lenses, including shared fields (name, description, role, confidence,
source_urls) and lens-specific fields per lens type.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] LensCandidate type defines shared fields: id, lens, name, description, role, confidence, source_urls
2. [unit] LensType enum includes all 7 lenses: capability_mapping, effort_elimination, info_asymmetry, decision_enablement, state_transitions, time_compression, artifact_creation
3. [unit] Lens-specific optional fields defined: enabling_features, effort_eliminated, information_gained, decision_enabled, state_transition, time_compression, artifact_type
4. [unit] ConfidenceLevel type includes: high, medium, low
5. [unit] LensResult type wraps array of candidates with lens metadata
6. [integration] Schema exports are importable from convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts following the pattern from extractActivationLevels.ts.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Flat optional fields over discriminated union — LLM output can't be trusted at the type level, validation belongs in the validation pass
- LensCandidate is a staging format, not a contract — downstream ValidatedCandidate has deliberately different shape
- Standalone types with no cross-module imports between lenses/types.ts and convergence/types.ts

### Product Perspective
- N/A — this is a pure types/schema task with no user-facing decisions

### Simplification Review
- Removed AllLensesResult — not in acceptance criteria, belongs with orchestrator story
- Kept all lens-specific optional fields and ConfidenceLevel type (required by ACs)
- Design is minimal: 4 exports (LensType, ConfidenceLevel, LensCandidate, LensResult) in one file

### Final Design
Single file `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` with:
- `LensType` union of 7 string literals
- `ConfidenceLevel` union: "high" | "medium" | "low"
- `LensCandidate` interface with 7 shared fields + 7 optional lens-specific fields
- `LensResult` wrapping candidates array with lens metadata

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover:
1. Create the types file
2. Write type-level tests (compile-time checks + runtime shape validation)
3. Verify exports are importable from the expected path

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*
*Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-07-lens-candidate-schema-design.md*

---
*Product Story: M003-E001-S001 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Create `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` with four type exports (`LensType`, `ConfidenceLevel`, `LensCandidate`, `LensResult`) that define the common output structure for all 7 analytical lenses. Write a companion test file that validates type shapes, field presence, and importability. This follows the same pattern used by `extractActivationLevels.ts` for type definitions and `extractActivationLevels.test.ts` for type-level testing.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` exports `LensCandidate` interface with fields `id: string`, `lens: LensType`, `name: string`, `description: string`, `role: string`, `confidence: ConfidenceLevel`, `source_urls: string[]`
- [ ] AC2: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` exports `LensType` as a union of exactly 7 string literals: `"capability_mapping" | "effort_elimination" | "info_asymmetry" | "decision_enablement" | "state_transitions" | "time_compression" | "artifact_creation"`
- [ ] AC3: `LensCandidate` interface includes 7 optional lens-specific fields: `enabling_features?: string[]`, `effort_eliminated?: string`, `information_gained?: string`, `decision_enabled?: string`, `state_transition?: string`, `time_compression?: string`, `artifact_type?: string`
- [ ] AC4: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` exports `ConfidenceLevel` as `"high" | "medium" | "low"`
- [ ] AC5: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` exports `LensResult` interface with fields `lens: LensType`, `candidates: LensCandidate[]`, `candidate_count: number`, `execution_time_ms: number`
- [ ] AC6: Running `npm test -- --run convex/analysis/lenses/types.test.ts` exits with code 0 and all tests pass

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create lenses directory**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/` (new directory)
   - Change: Create the directory to house lens-related modules

2. **Create types file**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts`
   - Change: Define and export 4 types following the design doc at `docs/plans/2026-02-07-lens-candidate-schema-design.md`:
     - `LensType` — union of 7 string literals
     - `ConfidenceLevel` — union of "high" | "medium" | "low"
     - `LensCandidate` — interface with 7 shared fields + 7 optional lens-specific fields
     - `LensResult` — interface with lens, candidates, candidate_count, execution_time_ms
   - Pattern reference: Follow `convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.ts` lines 8-30 for type definition style (exported types, no JSDoc noise, inline comments for context)

3. **Create test file**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Write vitest tests following the pattern from `convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.test.ts` lines 1-75:
     - Test LensType accepts all 7 values and verify the count
     - Test ConfidenceLevel accepts "high", "medium", "low"
     - Test LensCandidate with all shared fields populated
     - Test LensCandidate with each lens-specific optional field
     - Test LensCandidate with no optional fields (all undefined)
     - Test LensResult with lens metadata and candidates array
     - Import test: verify all 4 types are importable from `./types`

### Test Plan
- [ ] Verify AC1: Test creates a `LensCandidate` object with all 7 shared fields and asserts each field has correct value
- [ ] Verify AC2: Test creates an array of all 7 `LensType` values and asserts length is 7
- [ ] Verify AC3: Test creates `LensCandidate` objects with each optional lens-specific field and asserts the field is present; also tests a candidate with no optional fields
- [ ] Verify AC4: Test creates an array of all 3 `ConfidenceLevel` values and asserts length is 3
- [ ] Verify AC5: Test creates a `LensResult` with lens, candidates array, candidate_count, and execution_time_ms and asserts all fields
- [ ] Verify AC6: `npm test -- --run convex/analysis/lenses/types.test.ts` exits with code 0

description:
## Story: M005-E002-S002

**Epic:** M005-E002 - Profile Page Shell & Value Moments

## Outcome
A ValueMomentsSection component displays value moments from convergence results. Moments grouped by tier (Core, Important, Supporting) with stats bar and lens badges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [unit] Stats bar shows total moments and tier 1/2/3 counts
- [unit] Moments grouped into Tier 1 (Core), Tier 2 (Important), Tier 3 (Supporting) sections
- [unit] Each moment card shows name, description, lens badges, roles, and product surfaces

description:
## Context

**Epic M004-E001:** Output Types Schema

**Story M004-E001-S001:** Define output types for ICP, ActivationMap, and MeasurementSpec

## Specification

TypeScript types define the structure for all three output artifacts: ICPProfile, ActivationMap, and MeasurementSpec. Types reference existing ValueMoment and ActivationLevel types where appropriate.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] ICPProfile type includes: id, name, description, value_moment_priorities[], activation_triggers[], pain_points[], success_metrics[]
2. [unit] ValueMomentPriority type includes: moment_id, priority (1-3), relevance_reason
3. [unit] ActivationMap type includes: stages[], transitions[], value_unlocks[]
4. [unit] ActivationStage type includes: level, name, trigger_events[], value_moments_unlocked[], drop_off_risk
5. [unit] MeasurementSpec type includes: events[], metadata
6. [unit] TrackingEvent type includes: name, properties[], trigger_condition, maps_to (moment_id or activation_level)
7. [unit] All output types include confidence: number and sources: string[]
8. [integration] Types are exportable from convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts
- convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.ts

---
*Product Story: M004-E001-S001 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Types define the "shape of value" — what users ultimately receive from the analysis pipeline
- Each output artifact is a different lens: who uses the product (ICP), how they progress (activation map), what to measure (measurement spec)

### Technical Perspective
- Follow handoff hint over AC summaries — handoff provides downstream-consumption shapes
- Value unlocks embedded in stages (not separate top-level array) — avoids duplication, matches existing ActivationLevel patterns
- MeasurementSpec uses flat peer fields — no metadata wrapper object
- maps_to uses properly discriminated union with required fields per case (no optional ambiguity)
- Include primary_activation_level on ActivationMap as lightweight denormalization

### Simplification Review
- Removed EventProperty.required field — let TypeScript handle optionality
- Flattened coverage wrapper — activation_levels_covered and value_moments_covered are peer fields
- Tightened maps_to discriminated union — required fields per variant

### Final Design
Single file at convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts with pure TypeScript interfaces:
- 2 ICP types (ValueMomentPriority, ICPProfile)
- 3 Activation Map types (ActivationStage, StageTransition, ActivationMap)
- 3 Measurement Spec types (EventProperty, TrackingEvent, MeasurementSpec)
- 1 container type (OutputGenerationResult)
- Re-exports of upstream types for convenience

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover exact field types, import paths, and companion test file structure.

**Design doc:** docs/plans/2026-02-08-output-types-schema-design.md

---

title: Implement Batch 2 lenses (Info, Decision, State)
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E001:** Lens Execution Pipeline - Build 7 analytical lenses

**Story M003-E001-S003:** Implement 3 inference-heavy lenses that benefit from Batch 1 context.

**Dependencies:** Requires S001 (lens schema) to exist.

## Specification

Three lens extractors implemented as Convex internalActions: Information Asymmetry,
Decision Enablement, and State Transitions. These lenses are more inference-heavy
and benefit from having Batch 1 results as additional context.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] extractInfoAsymmetry action exists with productId and optional batch1Results args
2. [unit] extractDecisionEnablement action exists with productId and optional batch1Results args
3. [unit] extractStateTransitions action exists with productId and optional batch1Results args
4. [integration] Each lens produces 8-20 candidates when run against Linear product
5. [integration] Info Asymmetry populates information_gained field on candidates
6. [integration] Decision Enablement populates decision_enabled field on candidates
7. [integration] State Transitions populates state_transition field on candidates
8. [integration] Lenses produce higher quality output when batch1Results are provided
9. [integration] All candidates have source_urls linking to crawled pages

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/lenses/shared.ts for common utilities, then three lens files following the extractActivationLevels.ts pattern:
- convex/analysis/lenses/extractInfoAsymmetry.ts
- convex/analysis/lenses/extractDecisionEnablement.ts
- convex/analysis/lenses/extractStateTransitions.ts

These lenses find the "why" behind the "what":
- Info Asymmetry: What do users learn they couldn't know before?
- Decision Enablement: What decisions become possible?
- State Transitions: What user states change?

Optional batch1Results parameter injects capability and effort context.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.ts
- convex/productProfiles.ts

---
*Product Story: M003-E001-S003 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- These three lenses capture the transformation narrative: knowledge gaps filled, decisions unlocked, states changed
- Each lens should produce specific, product-contextualized candidates (not generic platitudes)
- Confidence scoring tied to evidence directness from crawled content

### Technical Perspective
- Hybrid approach: shared utilities for identical code (truncation, page context, response parsing), standalone files for domain logic
- Three self-contained internalActions with lens-specific prompts, page types, and knowledge graph context
- batch1Results typed as v.any() with runtime validation (pragmatic given S001 in-progress)

### Simplification Review
- Extracted truly identical utilities into shared.ts (truncateContent, buildPageContext, parseLensResponse)
- Rejected premature abstractions: no centralized model config, no Convex union types, no orchestrator-level context building
- Each lens file stays focused on its domain: page types, system prompt, knowledge context, action wiring

### Final Design
4 files: shared.ts + 3 lens extractors. Each lens ~100-120 lines focused on domain logic. Shared module handles mechanical concerns (truncation, formatting, JSON parsing). Claude Sonnet at temp 0.2 for precision. Results returned as LensResult, not persisted.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: shared.ts first, then three lenses in parallel, then tests.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-07-batch2-lenses-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Implement three inference-heavy lens extractors (Info Asymmetry, Decision Enablement, State Transitions) as Convex internalActions in `convex/analysis/lenses/`. These lenses analyze crawled product pages through "why" perspectives, optionally enriched with Batch 1 "what" results. A shared utility module handles content truncation, page context formatting, and JSON response parsing. Each lens file is self-contained with its own page type filtering, system prompt, and knowledge graph context builder.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractInfoAsymmetry.ts` exports an `internalAction` named `extractInfoAsymmetry` with args `{ productId: v.id("products"), batch1Results: v.optional(v.any()) }`
- [ ] AC2: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractDecisionEnablement.ts` exports an `internalAction` named `extractDecisionEnablement` with args `{ productId: v.id("products"), batch1Results: v.optional(v.any()) }`
- [ ] AC3: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractStateTransitions.ts` exports an `internalAction` named `extractStateTransitions` with args `{ productId: v.id("products"), batch1Results: v.optional(v.any()) }`
- [ ] AC4: `convex/analysis/lenses/shared.ts` exports `truncateContent`, `buildPageContext`, `parseLensResponse`, `callClaude`, and `extractJson` functions
- [ ] AC5: Each lens's `parseLensResponse` call validates its lens-specific field (`information_gained`, `decision_enabled`, `state_transition` respectively) exists on each candidate
- [ ] AC6: Unit tests in `convex/analysis/lenses/extractInfoAsymmetry.test.ts` verify page filtering selects `features, customers, help, homepage, solutions` types and `parseLensResponse` validates `information_gained` field
- [ ] AC7: Unit tests in `convex/analysis/lenses/extractDecisionEnablement.test.ts` verify page filtering selects `features, solutions, customers, homepage` types and `parseLensResponse` validates `decision_enabled` field
- [ ] AC8: Unit tests in `convex/analysis/lenses/extractStateTransitions.test.ts` verify page filtering selects `customers, features, onboarding, help, homepage` types and `parseLensResponse` validates `state_transition` field
- [ ] AC9: Unit tests in `convex/analysis/lenses/shared.test.ts` verify `truncateContent` truncates at line boundaries, `buildPageContext` respects per-page and total limits, `extractJson` handles code fences and raw JSON, and `parseLensResponse` assigns UUIDs and normalizes confidence
- [ ] AC10: `npm test` exits with code 0

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create lenses directory and types dependency**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/` (create directory)
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts` (create if S001 not yet merged)

2. **Create shared utility module**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/shared.ts`
   - Change: Implement 5 functions:
     - `truncateContent(content: string, max: number): string` — truncates at last newline before max, appends `[Content truncated]`
     - `buildPageContext(pages: Array<{pageType: string, content: string, url: string, title?: string}>, maxPerPage: number, maxTotal: number): string` — formats pages with URL headers, respects both limits
     - `extractJson(text: string): unknown` — handles `json` code fences and raw JSON
     - `parseLensResponse(responseText: string, lensType: LensType, lensField: string): LensCandidate[]` — calls `extractJson`, validates shared fields (name, description, role, confidence, source_urls), validates lens-specific field exists, assigns crypto.randomUUID() as id, normalizes confidence to ConfidenceLevel
     - `callClaude(options: { system: string, user: string, model?: string, temperature?: number, maxTokens?: number }): Promise<string>` — wraps Anthropic SDK, defaults to `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`, temp 0.2, max_tokens 4096
   - Constants: `MAX_CONTENT_PER_PAGE = 15_000`, `MAX_TOTAL_CONTENT = 40_000` (exported)

3. **Create extractInfoAsymmetry lens**

     - `PAGE_TYPES = ["features", "customers", "help", "homepage", "solutions"]`
     - `buildKnowledgeContext(profile)` extracting identity, entities, revenue sections
     - `buildBatch1Context(batch1Results)` formatting Batch 1 candidates as context string
     - System prompt asking: "What do users learn they couldn't know before?" with 3 examples, JSON schema requiring `information_gained` field, confidence rules, anti-patterns
     - Handler: fetch pages → filter by PAGE_TYPES → fetch profile → build contexts → callClaude → parseLensResponse("info_asymmetry", "information_gained") → return LensResult

4. **Create extractDecisionEnablement lens**

   - Change: Implement internalAction with:
     - `PAGE_TYPES = ["features", "solutions", "customers", "homepage"]`
     - `buildKnowledgeContext(profile)` extracting identity, entities, journey sections
     - `buildBatch1Context(batch1Results)` formatting Batch 1 candidates as context string

     - Handler: same flow as Info Asymmetry but with lens-specific config → parseLensResponse("decision_enablement", "decision_enabled") → return LensResult

5. **Create extractStateTransitions lens**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractStateTransitions.ts`
   - Change: Implement internalAction with:
     - `PAGE_TYPES = ["customers", "features", "onboarding", "help", "homepage"]`
     - `buildKnowledgeContext(profile)` extracting identity, entities, journey, activation definitions
     - `buildBatch1Context(batch1Results)` formatting Batch 1 candidates as context string
     - System prompt asking: "What user states change?" with 3 examples, JSON schema requiring `state_transition` field, confidence rules, anti-patterns
     - Handler: same flow → parseLensResponse("state_transitions", "state_transition") → return LensResult

6. **Write shared.ts unit tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/shared.test.ts`
   - Change: Tests for:
     - `truncateContent`: content shorter than max returns unchanged; long content truncates at last newline before max; appends truncation marker
     - `buildPageContext`: formats pages with headers; respects MAX_CONTENT_PER_PAGE; stops when MAX_TOTAL_CONTENT reached; handles empty pages array
     - `extractJson`: extracts from ```json fences; extracts from ``` fences; parses raw JSON; throws on invalid JSON
     - `parseLensResponse`: parses valid response with all fields; assigns UUIDs; validates lens-specific field exists (throws if missing); handles missing shared fields; normalizes confidence strings to ConfidenceLevel

7. **Write extractInfoAsymmetry unit tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractInfoAsymmetry.test.ts`
   - Change: Tests for:
     - Page filtering: only includes features, customers, help, homepage, solutions page types
     - Page filtering: excludes pricing, about, other types
     - Knowledge context: includes identity and entities and revenue sections from profile
     - Batch1 context: formats batch1Results into readable context string; handles undefined batch1Results gracefully
     - Response parsing: validates information_gained field present on each candidate

8. **Write extractDecisionEnablement unit tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/lenses/extractDecisionEnablement.test.ts`
   - Change: Tests for:
     - Page filtering: only includes features, solutions, customers, homepage types

### Technical Perspective
- TF-IDF + cosine similarity chosen over OpenAI embeddings or Haiku pairwise — zero API calls, deterministic, sub-100ms
- Smoothed IDF formula handles small corpora where standard IDF gives zero weight to shared terms
- Union-find with canMerge() pre-check prevents transitive same-lens violations
- Pure function architecture (clusterCandidatesCore) enables direct unit testing without Convex dependencies

### Product Perspective
- Clustering quality driven by downstream use case (tiering/convergence scoring)
- For short descriptions (10-30 words), lexical overlap via TF-IDF is sufficient
- No need for deep semantic understanding at this stage

- Every component earns its place

title: Implement LLM-powered activation map generator
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E003:** Activation Map Generation

**Story M004-E003-S002:** Implement LLM-powered activation map generator

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E003-S001 (activation data aggregation)

## Specification

An internalAction uses Claude to generate an activation map from aggregated activation levels and value moments, producing stages with trigger events, value unlocks, and drop-off risk indicators.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] generateActivationMap internalAction accepts productId and returns ActivationMap
2. [unit] Prompt includes activation levels and value moment suggestions
3. [unit] LLM output is parsed into ActivationMap structure
4. [unit] Stages array length matches activation levels count
5. [integration] Each stage has trigger_events derived from activation criteria
6. [integration] Value moments are distributed across stages (not all at one stage)
7. [integration] Drop-off risks are identified for at least one stage
8. [integration] Linear test produces 4-stage map with cycle completion as primary

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/analysis/lenses/shared.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- The activation map represents user transformation, not feature usage. L3 (cycle completion) is the "aha moment" where core value is realized
- Value moments distribute by tier: Tier 3 at early stages, Tier 1 at strong-signal stages where team outcomes become visible
- Biggest drop-off risk is L1→L2 (individual to team adoption); stages requiring behavior change are medium-high risk
- Linear test: 4 stages (explorer→workflow_learner→workflow_optimizer→product_workflow_master), primary at L3

### Technical Perspective
- Follow self-contained data fetching pattern from extractActivationLevels.ts — generator owns its data retrieval
- Use existing callClaude and extractJson utilities from lenses/shared.ts
- Don't couple to aggregateActivationInputs (S001) at runtime — dependency specifies what data is needed, not how

### Simplification Review
- Removed computeSuggestedMappings() — let Claude handle tier-to-level mapping from raw data
- Removed ActivationMapInput wrapper and preAggregatedData parameter
- Reduced exports to generateActivationMap and testGenerateActivationMap only
- Bake distribution/drop-off constraints into prompt as hard requirements

### Final Design
Single file: convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts
1. Fetch profile → extract activation levels + value moments
2. Build prompt (system rules + user data)
3. Call Claude Sonnet via callClaude(), temperature 0.2
4. Parse response via extractJson() + validation
5. Store result on product profile

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover:
- ActivationMap types (depends on M004-E001-S001)
- System prompt with stage/transition/distribution rules
- Parser with field validation
- Linear fixture test data
- Integration with profile storage

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-08-activation-map-generator-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Implement `generateActivationMap`, an LLM-powered internalAction that reads activation levels and value moments from a product profile, sends them to Claude Sonnet with a structured prompt, parses the response into an `ActivationMap` structure (stages, transitions, primary activation, drop-off risks), and stores the result back on the profile. The file follows the established self-contained pattern from `extractActivationLevels.ts`, using `callClaude` and `extractJson` from `lenses/shared.ts`.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts` exports `generateActivationMap` internalAction that accepts `{ productId: Id<"products"> }` and returns an object with `stages` array, `transitions` array, `primary_activation_level` number, `confidence` string, and `sources` array
- [ ] AC2: The `ACTIVATION_MAP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` string constant contains the words "activation levels", "value moments", "trigger_events", "drop_off_risk", and "stages"
- [ ] AC3: `parseActivationMapResponse(text)` returns a valid `ActivationMap` from JSON (raw or code-fenced) and throws descriptive errors for missing/invalid fields
- [ ] AC4: Given a Linear-fixture LLM response with 4 stages, `parseActivationMapResponse` returns `stages.length === 4`
- [ ] AC5: `parseActivationMapResponse` validates each stage has a non-empty `trigger_events` array (derived from activation criteria)
- [ ] AC6: `parseActivationMapResponse` validates that `value_moments_unlocked` arrays are not all concentrated on a single stage (at least 2 stages have value moments)
- [ ] AC7: `parseActivationMapResponse` validates at least one stage has `drop_off_risk.level` of `"medium"` or `"high"`
- [ ] AC8: Running `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.test.ts` exits with code 0

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Define ActivationMap types**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts`
   - Define types locally (until M004-E001-S001 lands `outputs/types.ts`):
     - `ActivationMapStage` — `{ level: number, name: string, signal_strength: string, trigger_events: string[], value_moments_unlocked: string[], drop_off_risk: { level: string, reason: string } }`
     - `ActivationMapTransition` — `{ from_level: number, to_level: number, trigger_events: string[], typical_timeframe?: string }`
     - `ActivationMap` — `{ stages: ActivationMapStage[], transitions: ActivationMapTransition[], primary_activation_level: number, confidence: string, sources: string[] }`

2. **Write system prompt constant**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts`
   - Export `ACTIVATION_MAP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` string constant
   - Include instructions for: one stage per activation level, derive trigger_events from criteria, distribute value moments by tier (T3→weak stages, T1→strong stages), assess drop-off risks, define transitions with timeframes, mark primary_activation_level as the aha moment
   - Include JSON schema example in the prompt

3. **Write prompt builder helper**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts`
   - `buildActivationMapUserPrompt(levels: ActivationLevel[], valueMoments: ValueMoment[], primaryActivation: number): string`
   - Format activation levels with criteria and signal strengths
   - Format value moments with tiers, roles, and descriptions
   - Include `primaryActivation` level number

4. **Write response parser**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts`
   - `parseActivationMapResponse(responseText: string): ActivationMap`
   - Use `extractJson` from `lenses/shared.ts` to unwrap code fences
   - Validate top-level fields: `stages` (array), `transitions` (array), `primary_activation_level` (number), `confidence` (string), `sources` (array)
   - Validate each stage: `level` (number), `name` (string), `signal_strength` (string), `trigger_events` (non-empty string[]), `value_moments_unlocked` (string[]), `drop_off_risk.level` (string), `drop_off_risk.reason` (string)
   - Validate each transition: `from_level` (number), `to_level` (number), `trigger_events` (string[])
   - Sort stages by level ascending
   - Validate `primary_activation_level` references an existing stage level

5. **Implement generateActivationMap internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts`
   - Import `{ internalAction }` from `../../_generated/server`, `{ internal }` from `../../_generated/api`, `{ v }` from `convex/values`
   - Import `{ callClaude, extractJson }` from `../lenses/shared`
   - Import `ActivationLevelsResult` type from `../extractActivationLevels`
   - Import `ConvergenceResult, ValueMoment` types from `../convergence/types`
   - Steps:
     1. Fetch profile via `ctx.runQuery(internal.productProfiles.getInternal, { productId })`
     2. Extract `definitions.activation` as `ActivationLevelsResult` and `convergence.value_moments` as `ValueMoment[]`
     3. Throw if either is missing
     4. Build user prompt via `buildActivationMapUserPrompt()`
     5. Call `callClaude({ system: ACTIVATION_MAP_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user: userPrompt, temperature: 0.2 })`
     6. Parse response via `parseActivationMapResponse()`
     7. Store result via `ctx.runMutation(internal.productProfiles.updateSectionInternal, { productId, section: "outputs", data: { ...existingOutputs, activationMap: result } })`
     8. Return the parsed `ActivationMap`

6. **Write test file with Linear fixture data**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.test.ts`
   - Import types and pure functions (parser, prompt builder, prompt constant)
   - **Type tests**: Verify `ActivationMap`, `ActivationMapStage`, `ActivationMapTransition` type shapes
   - **Prompt tests**: Verify `ACTIVATION_MAP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` contains key terms (stages, trigger_events, drop_off_risk, value moments, activation levels)
   - **Prompt builder tests**: Verify `buildActivationMapUserPrompt` formats levels and value moments, includes primaryActivation
   - **Parser tests** (using Linear fixture — 4 levels, explorer→workflow_learner→workflow_optimizer→product_workflow_master):
     - Parses raw JSON and code-fenced JSON
     - Returns 4 stages matching activation levels count
     - Each stage has non-empty trigger_events
     - Value moments distributed across at least 2 stages
     - At least one stage has medium or high drop_off_risk
     - primary_activation_level = 3
     - Throws on missing fields (stages, transitions, primary_activation_level, confidence, sources)
     - Throws on invalid stage (missing level, name, trigger_events)
     - Throws when primary_activation_level doesn't match any stage
     - Sorts stages by level ascending

### Test Plan

- [ ] Verify AC1: Test file imports `generateActivationMap` and type-checks the internalAction signature (type tests compile)
- [ ] Verify AC2: Test asserts `ACTIVATION_MAP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` contains "activation levels", "value moments", "trigger_events", "drop_off_risk", "stages"
- [ ] Verify AC3: Parser tests cover raw JSON, code-fenced JSON, and throw cases for missing/invalid fields
- [ ] Verify AC4: Parser test with 4-stage Linear fixture asserts `result.stages.length === 4`
- [ ] Verify AC5: Parser test asserts each `stage.trigger_events.length > 0`
- [ ] Verify AC6: Parser test asserts `stages.filter(s => s.value_moments_unlocked.length > 0).length >= 2`
- [ ] Verify AC7: Parser test asserts `stages.some(s => ["medium", "high"].includes(s.drop_off_risk.level))`
- [ ] Verify AC8: Run `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.test.ts` and confirm exit code 0

description:
## Story: M006-E002-S001

Update ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT to distinguish core daily users from evaluators/buyers/marketing personas. Primary persona must represent who uses the product daily, not who the website promotes.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Prompt contains instruction to distinguish 'core daily users' from 'evaluators/buyers'
- Prompt instructs primary persona must be who uses the product daily
- Prompt contains guidance to weight Tier 1 value moments over content volume
- Confidence scores reflect product-usage data, not marketing prominence
- buildICPPrompt includes Prioritization Guidance section with top tier-1 roles
- Existing parseICPProfiles and buildICPPrompt tests pass unchanged

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- The real distinction is 'who the product is built for' vs 'who the website markets to' — not usage frequency
- Tier 1 value moments should do the heavy lifting in prioritization signals
- Weekly dashboard users are still real product users; compliance doc readers are not

### Technical Perspective
- Prioritization Guidance must sort roles by tier_1_count descending before slicing top 3
- Without explicit sort, Map insertion order makes 'top roles' non-deterministic
- Spread copy [`...roles`] avoids mutating input array

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer flagged potential duplication between system prompt and data-level guidance
- Kept both: acceptance criteria explicitly require Prioritization Guidance in buildICPPrompt
- LLM prompt engineering benefits from instruction + concrete data reinforcement

### Final Design
Two additive changes to generateICPProfiles.ts:
1. **ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT**: New 'Persona Prioritization' section — defines core daily user vs evaluator/buyer, ranking by Tier 1 count, confidence based on usage evidence
2. **buildICPPrompt**: New 'Prioritization Guidance' section — top 3 roles sorted by tier_1_count descending

Plus new tests in generateICPProfiles.test.ts. No changes to parseICPProfiles, types, or schema.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover exact insertion points in the prompt string and test helper setup (makeRoleInput).

title: M006-E001-S002: Refine tier assignment to produce target distribution
description:
## Story: M006-E001-S002

Lower tier thresholds (4+ lenses = T1, 2-3 = T2, 1 = T3) and add distribution capping (max 3 T1, max 20 T3) so convergence produces a useful tiered set.

### Acceptance Criteria
- assignTier with lowered thresholds: 4+ lenses = T1, 2-3 lenses = T2, 1 lens = T3
- Post-tier capping: if >3 T1, demote lowest lens-count T1s to T2
- Post-tier capping: if >20 T3, drop lowest-confidence ones
- ConvergenceResult stats accurately reflect final tier counts after capping
- Existing assignTier tests updated to match new thresholds

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts
- convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Use contributing_candidates.length as ranking signal for both T1 demotion and T3 dropping — already on ValueMoment, no extra data needed
- Do NOT add confidence field to ValueMoment — conflates candidate-level confidence with merged-moment quality
- lens_count as tiebreaker for equal candidate counts
- One consistent ranking philosophy: more corroboration = keep

### Simplification Review
- Inline constants with comments, not exported module-level constants
- One consistent ranking rule for both T1 and T3 (not asymmetric)
- Keep capTierDistribution as named function for testability, but 4-5 focused tests not 8
- Pipeline order: converge -> cap -> compute stats

### Final Design
1. Update assignTier: >=4 T1, >=2 T2, else T3
2. Add capTierDistribution pure function: max 3 T1 (demote excess to T2), max 20 T3 (drop excess)
3. Call capTierDistribution at end of convergeAndTier before returning
4. Update existing tests + add 4-5 capping tests

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should detail exact test cases and file changes

title: M007-E001-S003: Add marketing-language detection to candidate validation
description:
## Story: M007-E001-S003

**Mission:** M007 - Insight Quality
**Epic:** M007-E001 - Experiential Lens Extraction

## Outcome
The candidate validation pipeline catches and rewrites candidates that describe abstract business outcomes instead of user experiences. Marketing-language patterns are detected deterministically, flagged candidates are sent to LLM for experiential rewrite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- MARKETING_LANGUAGE_PATTERNS constant added with regex for: automate, streamline, optimize, leverage, enhance, empower, accelerate, revolutionize, transform
- ABSTRACT_OUTCOME_PATTERNS constant for: at scale, cross-functional, end-to-end, enterprise-grade, best-in-class, next-generation
- New isMarketingLanguage() function detects marketing patterns without specific product surface references
- Flagged candidates sent to LLM review with experiential rewrite instruction
- Tests: "Automate protection of sensitive business information" IS flagged
- Tests: "Set board-level permissions to restrict editing" is NOT flagged
- Tests: "Streamline cross-functional collaboration workflows" IS flagged
- Full validation pipeline runs without errors

## Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts (existing patterns at lines 15-44)

## Implementation Hints
Follow same pattern as existing FEATURE_AS_VALUE_PATTERNS and VAGUE_PHRASES.
isMarketingLanguage() should NOT flag if candidate also references a specific product surface.
LLM rewrite uses same flow as feature-as-value rewrite (lines 379-452).

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Single function with one responsibility: "marketing smoke without product substance"
- Check both pattern lists (marketing verbs + abstract phrases) in single pass, return whichever hits first
- Escape hatch: substring match against knownFeatures, same approach as existing isVagueCandidate()
- No changes to LLM prompt or types — existing rewrite infrastructure handles flagged candidates generically

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer suggested merging pattern lists and removing escape hatch — rejected because ACs explicitly require two named constants and the NOT-flagged test case depends on escape hatch
- No new files, types, or abstractions — everything stays in existing validateCandidates.ts
- Single-line pipeline integration

### Final Design
Add MARKETING_LANGUAGE_PATTERNS (9 regex), ABSTRACT_OUTCOME_PATTERNS (6 strings), isMarketingLanguage(name, description, knownFeatures) function. Integrate with one line in runValidationPipeline(). Flagged candidates flow through existing applyLlmReview().

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: (1) constants, (2) isMarketingLanguage function, (3) pipeline integration line, (4) unit tests + integration test
